title: Periorbital skin rash
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.
assessment.rationale: The exact periocular-dermatitis discussion supports documenting eyelid or periorbital papules, pustules, redness, scale, itch, burning or soreness and corticosteroid-eye-ointment exposure only when assessed; it does not cover all causes of a periorbital rash.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with rosacea 2021

referral.text: Supports clinician documentation of facial distribution, erythema, papules or pustules, flushing, burning or stinging, dryness, edema, and ocular symptoms without inferring diagnosis.
referral.rationale: Ocular and periocular symptom context was reviewed, but no ocular diagnosis, medication, or referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician documentation of facial distribution, erythema, papules or pustules, flushing, burning or stinging, dryness, edema, and ocular symptoms without inferring diagnosis.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with rosacea 2021

exact_section.heading: Recommendations R3–R4 and background — clinical signs, symptoms, distribution, and ocular context

sources_used.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with rosacea 2021
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Vision, eye pain, photophobia, discharge, swelling, laterality, precise morphology, contact and medicine exposure, diagnosis, investigations, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
